div.h2: Lock + Wait Queue: Read/Write Critical Sections

p.strong: RULE: Never hold mutex while sleeping in wait_event_interruptible

g.text: Red border / fill | Critical section — mutex HELD
DEADLOCK SCENARIO: What happens if you hold mutex during sleep
BAD: Reader holds mutex, then sleeps
T=0 | Reader: mutex_lock() succeeds — Reader OWNS mutex
T=1 | Reader: buffer empty → enters wait_event (SLEEPING, mutex HELD)
T=2 | Writer: copy_from_user() done → tries mutex_lock()
T=3 | Writer: BLOCKS — mutex owned by sleeping Reader
T=4 | Writer cannot call wake_up → Reader never wakes
T=∞ | DEADLOCK: both processes stuck forever
Invariant: always true inside critical section
resize()→ wake_up(both) | capacity may have changed
read() + empty buffer | blocking: sleep | O_NONBLOCK: -EAGAIN
write() + full buffer | blocking: sleep | O_NONBLOCK: -EAGAIN
read() + data present | both: return data | (no difference)
write() + space present | both: write data | (no difference)
wait_event(read_queue, // Reader SLEEPS — still holds mutex!

text.tspan: // 2. check condition (no mutex needed for check)
// 3. schedule() — sleep WITHOUT holding any mutex
// 4. on wake: recheck condition under mutex
// 2. check condition (no mutex needed for check)
// 3. schedule() — sleep WITHOUT holding any mutex
// 4. on wake: recheck condition under mutex
// Reader A: wakes, acquires mutex, reads data
// Reader B: wakes, acquires mutex — buffer EMPTY!
// Reader A: wakes, acquires mutex, reads data
// Reader B: wakes, acquires mutex — buffer EMPTY!
